title: The QuickCrate app is crashing. What should I do?
body: If the QuickCrate app is crashing frequently, try these steps in order. First, force-close the app and reopen it — this clears temporary state issues. Second, check if there's an app update pending in the App Store or Google Play and install it, as crashes are often fixed in newer versions. Third, clear the app cache: on Android go to Settings → Apps → QuickCrate → Clear Cache. On iOS, offload and reinstall the app (Settings → General → iPhone Storage → QuickCrate → Offload App). Fourth, check your device storage — apps crash when the device is nearly full (less than 500 MB free). Fifth, if the crash happens on a specific screen (e.g., payment or tracking), take a screenshot of the error and report it via Help → Report a Bug. Our technical team reviews crash reports and releases patches typically within 48-72 hours of identifying a widespread issue.

title: I'm not receiving OTPs for login. What should I do?
body: If you're not receiving OTPs, first check that your phone has network signal and the number entered is your registered QuickCrate number. OTPs are sent via SMS — check if your phone has an SMS block active (some Android phones block unknown sender SMS by default; go to Messages → Settings → Spam Protection and whitelist QuickCrate's sender ID). If you're using a dual-SIM phone, ensure the OTP SIM is the primary SIM or the same one associated with your QuickCrate account. Wait at least 60 seconds before requesting a new OTP. If OTPs are still not arriving, try calling support on our helpline — agents can trigger an OTP to your email if registered. Network operator issues (DND mode, SMS routing delays) can occasionally cause delays of 5-10 minutes. If the problem persists for more than 15 minutes, contact support.

title: How do I update the QuickCrate app?
body: QuickCrate releases app updates regularly to add features, improve performance, and fix bugs. To update: on Android, open the Google Play Store, tap your profile → Manage apps and device → Tap QuickCrate → Update. On iOS, open the App Store, tap your profile (top right) → scroll down to see pending updates → tap Update next to QuickCrate. You can also enable automatic updates so you always have the latest version: on Android, in Play Store → Manage apps → QuickCrate → Enable Auto-update. On iOS, Settings → App Store → App Updates (toggle on). QuickCrate recommends always running the latest version for the best experience and security. Some features (e.g., new payment methods, dark-store tracking improvements) are only available in newer app versions. If an update fails to install, ensure you have at least 200 MB of free device storage.

title: Why is my QuickCrate app running slowly?
body: A slow QuickCrate app is often due to accumulated cache data, a poor network connection, or an older device with limited RAM. To speed things up: clear the app cache (Android: Settings → Apps → QuickCrate → Clear Cache; iOS: offload and reinstall the app). Check your internet connection — QuickCrate's real-time tracking and catalog images require a stable connection; switch from Wi-Fi to 4G or vice versa to test. Close other background apps to free up RAM. Restart your phone if it hasn't been restarted in several days. If the app is slow specifically during map-based tracking, this is typically a location service issue — ensure Location Services are enabled and set to 'Always On' or 'While Using App' for QuickCrate. For persistent slowness on older devices, the QuickCrate Lite mode (Settings → App Experience → Lite Mode) disables some animations and catalog images for faster performance.

title: How do I enable location services for QuickCrate?
body: QuickCrate uses your location for two key purposes: auto-detecting your delivery address and enabling real-time delivery tracking. On Android, go to Settings → Apps → QuickCrate → Permissions → Location and select 'Allow while using app' or 'Always allow'. On iOS, go to Settings → Privacy → Location Services → QuickCrate and select 'While Using App' or 'Always'. If you deny location access, you can still use the app by manually entering your delivery address, but the real-time map tracking feature will not work. After enabling location, you may need to reopen the app for the change to take effect. QuickCrate does not track your location in the background when you're not using the app (regardless of the permission setting, we only query your location during an active session or active delivery). Your location data is used solely for service delivery and is not sold.

title: Can I use the QuickCrate website instead of the app?
body: Yes, QuickCrate has a fully functional website at quickcrate.in that mirrors all app features, including browsing, cart, checkout, order tracking, account management, and customer support. The website is optimized for both desktop and mobile browsers. All your account data (orders, addresses, wallet, rewards) is synced between the app and website in real time — you can place an order on the website and track it in the app, or vice versa. Some app-exclusive features (like the barcode scanner, biometric PIN lock, and push notifications) are not available on the website. For the fastest experience, especially for order tracking and flash sales, the app is recommended. The website uses the same secure login (OTP-based) as the app. Progressive Web App (PWA) installation is also supported — add quickcrate.in to your home screen from your mobile browser for an app-like experience without the download.

title: How do I report a bug or technical issue with the QuickCrate app?
body: To report a bug, go to Help → Report a Bug within the app. Describe the issue in detail: what you were doing when the bug occurred, which screen was affected, what happened vs. what you expected. Attach a screenshot if possible — this significantly speeds up diagnosis. You can also submit bug reports to bugs@quickcrate.in if you're unable to access the app. Our engineering team triages all bug reports. Critical issues affecting order placement or payment are addressed within 24 hours; non-critical UI or display bugs may take 3-7 days to fix. If your issue was causing financial impact (e.g., double charge due to a bug), mention it explicitly and our support team will prioritize resolution. We release hotfix updates for critical bugs without waiting for the regular weekly release cycle.

title: I accidentally logged out of the app. How do I log back in?
body: Logging back into QuickCrate is quick. Open the app and tap 'Log In'. Enter your registered mobile number and tap 'Send OTP'. Enter the 6-digit OTP sent to your phone and tap 'Verify'. You'll be returned to your account with all your data intact — orders, addresses, wallet balance, and rewards are stored server-side and not affected by logging out. If you've changed your phone number since you last logged in, enter the old number to access the account and then update your number in Account → Personal Details. If you're on a new device, you'll also need to complete device verification (OTP + security PIN). For security, QuickCrate logs you out automatically after 90 days of inactivity. Regular users don't experience this because the app keeps sessions active as long as it's opened periodically.

title: Why is my QuickCrate app showing incorrect prices or products?
body: If you see incorrect prices or products in the app, it is likely due to a stale app cache. Pull down to refresh the home screen or category page to force a data reload. If the issue persists, clear the app cache (Android: Settings → Apps → QuickCrate → Clear Cache) and reopen the app. Prices and availability are fetched from the server in real time, so after a refresh, you should see current data. In rare cases, a display bug may show an older cached price — the price at checkout is always the confirmed, server-side price. If the checkout price differs from what the listing showed, screenshot both and report via Help → Report a Bug — this helps us fix catalog sync issues. For product catalog errors (wrong product name, image, or description), use 'Report Listing Issue' on the product detail page.

title: How do I contact QuickCrate customer support?
body: QuickCrate offers multiple support channels. In-app chat: Go to Help → Chat with Us for real-time support with a human agent (average wait time under 3 minutes during business hours, 8 AM - 10 PM). Outside business hours, the chat bot can handle common queries and log urgent issues for next-morning review. Phone support: Call 1800-XXX-XXXX (toll-free) from 8 AM to 10 PM, 7 days a week. Email: For non-urgent issues, email help@quickcrate.in — responses within 24 hours. Social media: DM us on Twitter @QuickCrateSupport or Instagram @QuickCrateIndia for public or private queries. In-app issue reporting: For order-specific issues (wrong item, damaged goods, delivery problems), use 'My Orders → Report an Issue' for the fastest resolution, as this pre-fills your order details for the agent.
